- bugs found: GBrain MCP failed with `Connection closed` because `gbrain serve` could inherit repository-local database context from the current working directory, including Prisma-style `?schema=public` URLs that PostgreSQL rejected as `unrecognized configuration parameter "schema"`. Empty DB URL variables in MCP config also needed explicit stdio intent for the child runtime
- security risks found: GBrain MCP proxy now strips `DATABASE_URL` and `GBRAIN_DATABASE_URL` by default unless `GBRAIN_STDIO_PROXY_ALLOW_DB_ENV=1` is explicitly set, reducing accidental cross-repo DB credential/config leakage into the global brain server. Subagent prompts now explicitly require evidence-backed claims, preservation of user changes, validation result reporting, and medical/pharmacy/privacy boundary checks
- performance issues found: failed MCP startup previously spent about 12s per connection attempt before closing. The proxy now starts from the stable home context and `hermes mcp test gbrain` discovers tools in about 2.3s
- validation commands: `python3` TOML parse for user config/profile/agent files; `python3 -m py_compile /Users/yusuke/.hermes/scripts/mcp/gbrain_stdio_proxy.py`; `python3` import check for `_child_env`; direct proxy startup with a bad `DATABASE_URL`; `hermes mcp test gbrain`; `codex --version`; `codex mcp list`; `codex doctor --all`; focused `rg` confirmation for 0.136.0 prompt/config markers
- validation results: TOML parse passed for 40 files; proxy Python compile passed; `_child_env` strips DB URL variables by default and preserves them only with `GBRAIN_STDIO_PROXY_ALLOW_DB_ENV=1`; direct proxy no longer emits the schema DB error when isolated to home; `hermes mcp test gbrain` passed and discovered 88 tools; `codex --version` reports `codex-cli 0.136.0`; `codex mcp list` shows `gbrain` enabled with `MCP_STDIO`; `codex doctor --all` loaded config successfully and confirms current latest 0.136.0, with the remaining expected non-interactive terminal failure `TERM=dumb` plus stale-thread warnings

- bugs found: pharmacist credential create/update and pharmacist CSV import normalized numeric strings with `Number(trimmed)`, so exponent-style or malformed-looking strings such as `1e1` could be accepted as ordinary Float values before credential reference validation, staff import site reads, Cognito invites, membership creation, pharmacist-credential writes, or audit writes. The admin credential UI also converted number input text with `Number(...)` before POST/PATCH, which could erase the original operator input shape before server validation
- security risks found: pharmacist credential/import schemas now accept JSON numbers and plain decimal strings only for `tenure_years` and `weekly_work_hours`, while preserving blank-to-null behavior and Float bounds. ### 20260601-121533

- current task: harden patient MCS overview limit parsing at the route boundary and direct service boundary
- files inspected: `git status --short`, `.codex/ralph-state.md`, memory registry CareViaX continuation entries, Vercel Next.js skill instructions, `node_modules/next/dist/docs/01-app/03-api-reference/03-file-conventions/route.md`, `package.json`/repo validation locations, `src/app/api/patients/[id]/mcs/route.ts`, `src/app/api/patients/[id]/mcs/route.test.ts`, `src/server/services/patient-mcs.ts`, `src/server/services/patient-mcs.test.ts`, `src/lib/api/pagination.ts`, `src/lib/api/pagination.test.ts`, related patient-MCS DTO/query tests, focused diffs, and targeted validation output
- files changed: `src/lib/api/pagination.ts`, `src/lib/api/pagination.test.ts`, `src/app/api/patients/[id]/mcs/route.ts`, `src/app/api/patients/[id]/mcs/route.test.ts`, `src/server/services/patient-mcs.ts`, `src/server/services/patient-mcs.test.ts`, `.codex/ralph-state.md`
- bugs found: the patient MCS overview route still parsed `limit` with `Number(...)`, so blank query values and exponent-style numeric strings such as `1e2` could be accepted as valid message limits. Direct service callers could also pass oversized, negative, fractional, or non-finite `limit` values through to the MCS message query without a service-level bound
- security risks found: patient MCS overview now uses a shared strict optional bounded integer parser before loading the patient, assignment-scoped patient record, or sensitive MCS timeline data. Malformed/blank/exponent/out-of-range limits fail before PHI reads. Service-level message limit normalization now caps direct callers to `PATIENT_MCS_MAX_MESSAGE_LIMIT`, preserves `limit=0` as "do not load messages", and avoids leaking invalid numeric values into Prisma query options. Existing auth, sensitive-role gating, patient assignment scoping, route-param normalization, MCS host validation, and external scrape payload normalization were not relaxed
- performance issues found: malformed limits now stop before patient lookup and MCS overview loading, and direct oversized service limits are capped to 100 before querying timeline messages. This prevents typo-driven large MCS message fetches while preserving the default 50-message overview behavior
- validation commands: `pnpm exec prettier --write src/lib/api/pagination.ts src/lib/api/pagination.test.ts 'src/app/api/patients/[id]/mcs/route.ts' 'src/app/api/patients/[id]/mcs/route.test.ts' src/server/services/patient-mcs.ts src/server/services/patient-mcs.test.ts`; `pnpm --config.verify-deps-before-run=false exec vitest run src/lib/api/pagination.test.ts 'src/app/api/patients/[id]/mcs/route.test.ts' src/server/services/patient-mcs.test.ts src/lib/patient-mcs/dto.test.ts src/lib/patient-mcs/query.test.ts`; `pnpm --config.verify-deps-before-run=false exec eslint src/lib/api/pagination.ts src/lib/api/pagination.test.ts 'src/app/api/patients/[id]/mcs/route.ts' 'src/app/api/patients/[id]/mcs/route.test.ts' src/server/services/patient-mcs.ts src/server/services/patient-mcs.test.ts src/lib/patient-mcs/dto.ts src/lib/patient-mcs/dto.test.ts src/lib/patient-mcs/query.ts src/lib/patient-mcs/query.test.ts --max-warnings=0`; `pnpm --config.verify-deps-before-run=false exec tsc --noEmit --pretty false`; `git diff --check -- src/lib/api/pagination.ts src/lib/api/pagination.test.ts 'src/app/api/patients/[id]/mcs/route.ts' 'src/app/api/patients/[id]/mcs/route.test.ts' src/server/services/patient-mcs.ts src/server/services/patient-mcs.test.ts`; focused diff/status inspection

- security risks found: data-explorer row listing now treats optional pagination params as blank-or-strict digit strings, rejects malformed/exponent-like values before calling the row service, and rejects offsets above the shared maximum. Existing canAdmin authorization, org-scoped SQL predicates, allowlisted tables, denied-field projection, editable-field restrictions, row update request-body hardening, and model listing behavior were not relaxed
- performance issues found: route-level malformed/oversized pagination now stops before `listDataExplorerRows`, and service-level direct calls cap `limit` at 100 and `offset` at 999900 before composing SQL `LIMIT/OFFSET`, preventing accidental very large admin explorer scans

The dashboard now passes the visible report `updated_at` for triage, resolve, and task-conversion status changes
- performance issues found: malformed self-report payloads now exit before DB lookup/transaction work. Stale client updates and assignment-loss races stop before update work and result loading. No broad performance refactor was made

- current task: harden management-plan request boundaries, date invariants, version conflicts, and stale status/assignment writes
- files inspected: `git status --short`, `.codex/ralph-state.md`, memory registry lines for CareViaX continuation, `package.json`, `node_modules/next/dist/docs/01-app/03-api-reference/03-file-conventions/route.md`, `src/lib/api/request-body.ts`, `src/lib/api/request-body.test.ts`, `src/lib/api/response.ts`, `src/lib/db/json.ts`, `src/lib/auth/visit-schedule-access.ts`, `src/lib/db/rls.ts`, `src/lib/validations/management-plan.ts`, `src/app/api/management-plans/route.ts`, `src/app/api/management-plans/route.test.ts`, `src/app/api/management-plans/[id]/route.ts`, `src/app/api/management-plans/[id]/route.test.ts`, `src/server/services/management-plans.ts`, `src/server/services/management-plans.test.ts`, Prisma `ManagementPlan` schema, raw request-body parsing grep results, verifier output, API contract reviewer output, medical-safety reviewer output, and privacy reviewer output
- files changed: `src/lib/validations/management-plan.ts`, `src/app/api/management-plans/route.ts`, `src/app/api/management-plans/route.test.ts`, `src/app/api/management-plans/[id]/route.ts`, `src/app/api/management-plans/[id]/route.test.ts`, `.codex/ralph-state.md`
- bugs found: management-plan create/update still used local request-body parsing instead of the shared helper, malformed JSON was not locked before DB work, blank `case_id` query filters widened into all assigned plans, invalid calendar dates and review dates before effective dates could pass, concurrent create version conflicts could surface as generic server failures, and PATCH update/archive/approve used stale pre-read state with `id`-only writes and stale notification patient/assignee data
- security risks found: malformed/non-object management-plan payloads now fail through `readJsonObjectRequestBody` before case/plan lookup, transaction work, writes, or notification/task side effects. Blank `case_id` query parameters now return 400 instead of broadening into cross-case reads. PATCH mutations now re-check assignment/status inside the transaction and use guarded `updateMany` before returning 409 conflicts on stale assignment/status changes. Approval notification metadata now uses the transaction-time case patient/primary pharmacist values instead of stale pre-read values. Existing auth, org scoping, assignment predicates, response envelopes, and idempotent already-approved response behavior were not relaxed
- performance issues found: malformed/blank/invalid date payloads now stop before DB reads and transaction callbacks where possible. Create version conflicts now avoid generic error handling by mapping Prisma `P2002` to a bounded 409 response. Stale PATCH requests stop before notification scheduling/resolution side effects. No broad performance refactor was made
- validation commands: `pnpm exec prettier --write src/lib/validations/management-plan.ts src/app/api/management-plans/route.ts src/app/api/management-plans/route.test.ts 'src/app/api/management-plans/[id]/route.ts' 'src/app/api/management-plans/[id]/route.test.ts'`; `pnpm --config.verify-deps-before-run=false exec vitest run src/lib/api/request-body.test.ts src/app/api/management-plans/route.test.ts 'src/app/api/management-plans/[id]/route.test.ts' src/server/services/management-plans.test.ts`; `pnpm --config.verify-deps-before-run=false exec eslint src/lib/api/request-body.ts src/lib/api/request-body.test.ts src/lib/validations/management-plan.ts src/app/api/management-plans/route.ts src/app/api/management-plans/route.test.ts 'src/app/api/management-plans/[id]/route.ts' 'src/app/api/management-plans/[id]/route.test.ts' src/server/services/management-plans.ts src/server/services/management-plans.test.ts --max-warnings=0`; `pnpm --config.verify-deps-before-run=false exec tsc --noEmit --pretty false`; `git diff --check -- src/lib/validations/management-plan.ts src/app/api/management-plans/route.ts src/app/api/management-plans/route.test.ts 'src/app/api/management-plans/[id]/route.ts' 'src/app/api/management-plans/[id]/route.test.ts'`; `rg -n --glob 'route.ts' '\b(req|request)\.json\s*\(|\.json\s*\(\)\.catch|JSON\.parse' src/app/api/management-plans`; targeted grep for conflict/date/stale-assignment/blank-filter coverage

Auth permissions, org scoping, reference validation, RLS writes, and response envelopes were not relaxed

### 20260531-185403

- current task: harden patient status tracker audit-log status restoration before status-change audit and notification generation
- files inspected: `git status --short`, `.codex/ralph-state.md`, `src/server/services/patient-status-tracker.ts`, `src/types/dashboard-home.ts`, `src/lib/patient/status-icon.ts`, patient-risk call sites and current grep results for remaining production JSON/record casts
- files changed: `src/server/services/patient-status-tracker.ts`, `src/server/services/patient-status-tracker.test.ts`, `.codex/ralph-state.md`
- bugs found: `trackPatientStatusChanges()` read persisted audit-log `changes.to` through a direct `Record<string, unknown>` cast and then cast any string to `PatientStatusIcon`. A malformed latest audit log such as `{ to: "not_a_status" }` could become the previous status and later crash when indexing `STATUS_ICON_CONFIG[previousStatus]`
- security risks found: persisted audit-log changes now pass through `readJsonObject`, and `to` is accepted only when it is one of the known `PatientStatusIcon` values. Malformed or non-object audit-log rows are skipped, allowing the latest valid previous status to be used or falling back to `stable`. No risk-summary query, current-status derivation, audit-log write shape, notification trigger rules, notification payload, or patient link changed
- performance issues found: no meaningful performance issue found; validation is a small switch over one persisted `changes.to` value per prior audit-log row
- validation commands: `pnpm exec prettier --write src/server/services/patient-status-tracker.ts src/server/services/patient-status-tracker.test.ts`; `pnpm --config.verify-deps-before-run=false exec vitest run src/server/services/patient-status-tracker.test.ts`; `rg -n "log\\.changes as Record|readPatientStatusIcon|readJsonObject\\(log\\.changes\\)|not_a_status|not object-shaped" src/server/services/patient-status-tracker.ts src/server/services/patient-status-tracker.test.ts`; `pnpm --config.verify-deps-before-run=false exec eslint src/server/services/patient-status-tracker.ts src/server/services/patient-status-tracker.test.ts src/lib/db/json.ts src/lib/patient/status-icon.ts --max-warnings=0`; `git diff --check -- src/server/services/patient-status-tracker.ts src/server/services/patient-status-tracker.test.ts`; `pnpm --config.verify-deps-before-run=false exec tsc --noEmit --pretty false`

- bugs found: SAM authorizer still received the room-token signing secret as a raw environment value; app and Lambda secret parsing accepted JSON SecretString values without the expected `COLLABORATION_ROOM_TOKEN_SECRET` key as the signing secret; short/weak signing secrets were accepted after ARN migration; Lambda verifier did not have first-class tests for Secrets Manager success/failure/direct-env rejection; app production-like detection was initially tied to `NODE_ENV` instead of the repo's `APP_ENV` convention
- security risks found: reduced signing-key exposure by changing the SAM contract to pass only `COLLABORATION_ROOM_TOKEN_SECRET_ARN` and grant authorizer-scoped `secretsmanager:GetSecretValue`; reduced secret misconfiguration risk by requiring raw or JSON secret values to resolve to a trimmed 32+ character signing secret and rejecting the known local fallback value; Lambda validation now fails closed on Secrets Manager lookup failure, missing key, malformed JSON, short secret, and direct env values in Lambda-like runtimes; app issuer uses `APP_ENV`/`AWS_EXECUTION_ENV` production-like gating rather than `NODE_ENV`
- performance issues found: added a 5-minute in-process Secrets Manager cache for app and Lambda secret lookups, matching the room-token TTL to limit repeated AWS calls while bounding rotation drift
- validation commands: `pnpm --config.verify-deps-before-run=false add @aws-sdk/client-secrets-manager@^3.1050.0` exited 1 after updating dependencies because existing pnpm ignored-build policy requires approval; `pnpm --config.verify-deps-before-run=false install --lockfile-only --ignore-scripts`; Prettier on touched token/template/README/package files; targeted Vitest for app room-token, Lambda room-token, authorizer, and template tests; full WebSocket Lambda/template Vitest plus collaboration room-token service test; targeted ESLint for touched token/template tests and handlers; `pnpm --config.verify-deps-before-run=false exec tsc --noEmit --pretty false`; `git diff --check`; `pnpm --config.verify-deps-before-run=false test`; `pnpm --config.verify-deps-before-run=false lint`; `pnpm --config.verify-deps-before-run=false build`; `pnpm --config.verify-deps-before-run=false audit --audit-level high`; `command -v sam && command -v cfn-lint`

### 20260521-163520

- current task: continue WebSocket/Yjs hardening by fixing high-confidence sync fan-out reliability/performance issues, protocol rejection tests, and SAM throttling guardrails
- files inspected: `git status --short`, `.codex/ralph-state.md`, `tools/infra/websocket/lambdas/sync/handler.ts`, `tools/infra/websocket/lambdas/sync/handler.test.ts`, `tools/infra/websocket/lambdas/shared/connection-store.ts`, `tools/infra/websocket/lambdas/shared/connection-store.test.ts`, `tools/infra/websocket/template.yaml`, `tools/infra/websocket/template.test.ts`, `tools/infra/websocket/README.md`, `package.json`, `.github/workflows/ci.yml`, and subagent findings from performance-auditor, security-auditor, and test-auditor
- files changed: `tools/infra/websocket/lambdas/sync/handler.ts`, `tools/infra/websocket/lambdas/sync/handler.test.ts`, `tools/infra/websocket/template.yaml`, `tools/infra/websocket/template.test.ts`, `tools/infra/websocket/README.md`, `.codex/ralph-state.md`
- bugs found: sync fan-out had unbounded concurrency, then a bounded limiter that still stopped all remaining peer sends on the first non-Gone failure; expired-peer cleanup ran only after active peer fan-out fully succeeded; Gone cleanup failures were not isolated from other peer delivery; text frames and `messageAuth`/unknown Yjs frames were not explicitly covered; the WebSocket stage lacked route throttling/data-trace guardrails and authorizer/sync reserved concurrency
- security risks found: reduced DoS/cost exposure by adding explicit WebSocket route throttling, disabled API Gateway data tracing, detailed metrics, and authorizer/sync reserved concurrency parameters; reduced CRDT payload integrity risk by rejecting non-base64 text frames before reading the connection store; kept message auth and unknown Yjs frames rejected before DDB/API Gateway calls
- performance issues found: capped peer fan-out and expired cleanup concurrency at 10; changed peer delivery to continue after per-peer non-Gone failures instead of stopping the room-wide queue; made expired cleanup independent so stale records are removed even when active peer delivery has partial or total failures
- validation commands: Prettier on touched WebSocket files; targeted Vitest for `tools/infra/websocket/lambdas/sync/handler.test.ts`; targeted Vitest for WebSocket template/sync; full WebSocket Lambda/template Vitest plus collaboration room-token service test; targeted ESLint for WebSocket template/sync tests and handler; `pnpm --config.verify-deps-before-run=false exec tsc --noEmit --pretty false`; `git diff --check` for touched WebSocket files; `pnpm --config.verify-deps-before-run=false test`; `pnpm --config.verify-deps-before-run=false lint`; `pnpm --config.verify-deps-before-run=false build`; `pnpm --config.verify-deps-before-run=false audit --audit-level high`; `command -v sam`
- validation results: sync handler test passed 14 tests; WebSocket template/sync tests passed 22 tests; WebSocket Lambda/template suite passed 8 files / 49 tests; TypeScript passed; targeted ESLint passed; diff whitespace check passed; full Vitest passed 542 files / 2682 tests with existing jsdom navigation warnings only; full lint passed; Next.js 16.2.6 production build passed with 217 app routes; dependency audit reported no known vulnerabilities; `sam` CLI was still not installed, so SAM build/validate remains unrun
- remaining work: move room-token secret value out of Lambda env into Secrets Manager/SSM SecureString; add actual SAM/cfn-lint deployability gate and possibly Node 20 Lambda test matrix; add client token renewal before 5-minute expiry; consider current-authority revocation/version checks and API Gateway `DeleteConnectionCommand`; consider page-streamed room queries or room connection caps for very large rooms

- bugs found: collaborative Yjs provider could be constructed from raw `entityType:entityId` without calling the assignment-scoped token route; Yjs room naming differed between client and server helper; provider allowed tokenless construction and production plaintext `ws://`; remote CRDT map updates could apply unregistered form keys; the new collaboration room-token route was missing from protected-route and rate-limit catalogs
- security risks found: reduced PHI/IDOR risk by requiring `requireAuthContext`, assignment-scoped entity access, short-lived room-limited tokens, canonical `orgId:entityType:entityId` room names, and fail-closed provider construction before Yjs WebSocket connection; preserved client-session Cognito token non-exposure by using a room-limited token instead of a general bearer token
- performance issues found: none severe; token fetch is performed once per collaborative form/entity lifecycle and inaccessible entities fail before provider creation or WebSocket connection
- validation commands: targeted Vitest for collaboration access/token route/provider/hook/presence/org-realtime/auth config/protected route tests; `pnpm --config.verify-deps-before-run=false exec tsc --noEmit --pretty false`; `pnpm --config.verify-deps-before-run=false exec vitest run src/lib/api/rate-limit.test.ts`; `pnpm --config.verify-deps-before-run=false lint`; `pnpm --config.verify-deps-before-run=false audit --prod --audit-level moderate`; `pnpm --config.verify-deps-before-run=false exec prisma validate --schema=prisma/schema/`; `pnpm --config.verify-deps-before-run=false test`; `pnpm --config.verify-deps-before-run=false build`; `git diff --check`
- validation results: targeted collaboration/auth regression suite passed 9 files / 119 tests; TypeScript passed; rate-limit catalog test passed 1 file / 21 tests; lint passed; production dependency audit reported no known vulnerabilities; Prisma schema validation passed; full Vitest passed 535 files / 2635 tests with existing jsdom navigation warnings only; Next.js 16.2.6 production build passed with 217 app routes; diff whitespace check passed

- bugs found: `@sentry/nextjs` needed root-resolvable `@opentelemetry/instrumentation` for config/build; Sentry v10 emitted a deprecated top-level `reactComponentAnnotation` warning; offline crypto re-read IndexedDB keys per payload; active offline page cache name `pages` was also deleted as a legacy cache; prescription drafts, sync queue payloads/conflict payloads, and visit brief cache still used best-effort encryption and could fail open to plaintext PHI; external-access allowed `medication_list`/`allergy_info` scopes for `clerk`; file completion/download authorization did not re-run purpose-specific entity/assignment checks; complete upload did not compare S3 `ContentLength`/`ContentType` to expected metadata
- security risks found: reduced local PHI-at-rest exposure by requiring encrypted prescription draft, sync queue, conflict, SOAP draft, geolog, residual, and visit-brief cache payloads before IndexedDB writes; added migration coverage for legacy plaintext SOAP duplicate fields; reduced external PHI sharing by requiring `canVisit` for medication/allergy external scopes; reduced same-org file IDOR by enforcing purpose-specific assignment checks for complete/download/presigned-download; added S3 size/type verification during complete
- performance issues found: memoized offline CryptoKey in memory to avoid repeated IndexedDB key reads per encrypt/decrypt payload; separated current offline page cache as `offline-pages-v2` so Service Worker activation can delete legacy `pages` without wiping the active offline page cache
- validation commands: targeted Vitest for offline/external-access files; targeted files API/file-storage Vitest; `pnpm exec tsc --noEmit --pretty false`; `pnpm lint`; `pnpm test`; `pnpm build`; `pnpm audit --audit-level moderate`; `git diff --check`
- validation results: targeted offline/external-access Vitest passed with 8 files / 34 tests before files integration; targeted files API/file-storage Vitest passed with 5 files / 38 tests; final TypeScript passed; final ESLint passed; final full Vitest passed with 506 files / 2092 tests; final production build passed with no Sentry build warnings; dependency audit reported no known vulnerabilities; diff whitespace check passed
- remaining work: no failing validation remains. Full browser Playwright execution was not rerun in this final integration pass; browser-level shared viewer/offline flow smoke remains the main residual confidence gap. Performance auditor follow-ups not completed here include true bulk `visit-brief` projection, billing evidence bulk upsert/rules memoization, drug-master import index/caching, and Data Explorer search/count optimization.
- next action: run production Playwright smoke for shared external viewer URL/OTP behavior, offline save/cache behavior, and route-mocked billing workbench if browser-level confidence is required before commit

- files inspected: `git status --short --untracked-files=all`, `.codex/ralph-state.md`, `src/lib/auth/visit-schedule-access.ts`, dashboard assignment-scope helper/tests, management-plan routes/tests, task routes/tests, validation schemas, and four read-only assignment-scope subagent reports for management-plans, tasks, first-visit/interventions, and facility/external-professional APIs

### 20260521-105717

- current task: fix the High/High dispense barcode verification PHI exposure from the task subagent report
- files inspected: `git status --short --untracked-files=all`, `.codex/ralph-state.md`, `src/app/api/dispense-tasks/[id]/verify-barcode/route.ts`, its tests, `src/server/services/prescription-access.ts`, dispense task/detail/result/queue routes, and Prisma prescription/dispense task schema relations
- files changed: `src/app/api/dispense-tasks/[id]/verify-barcode/route.ts`, `src/app/api/dispense-tasks/[id]/verify-barcode/route.test.ts`, `.codex/ralph-state.md`
- bugs found: `POST /api/dispense-tasks/:id/verify-barcode` had no explicit `canDispense` permission, loaded the dispense task by `{ id, org_id }` only, loaded `PrescriptionLine` by `{ id, org_id }` only, and parsed the submitted barcode before proving the line belonged to the verified task/cycle; this could disclose expected drug code/name for another same-org patient's prescription line
- security risks found: reduced dispensing PHI IDOR by requiring `canDispense`, applying `buildMedicationCycleAssignmentWhere` to the task lookup, constraining `PrescriptionLine` to the verified task's `cycle_id`, and delaying GS1 barcode parsing until after task and line scope are proven
- performance issues found: no material performance issue changed; the line lookup adds a relational cycle predicate and avoids barcode parsing on denied requests

### 20260521-113810

- current task: fix high-confidence runtime/security findings for rate limiting, bulk-export audit/job isolation, and PH-OS audit migration coverage
- files inspected: `git status --short`, `.codex/ralph-state.md`, `src/lib/api/rate-limit.ts`, `src/lib/api/rate-limit.test.ts`, `src/proxy.ts`, `src/proxy.test.ts`, bulk export route/service/job/tests, job route/tests, `package.json`, `.github/workflows/ci.yml`, `prisma/migrations/20260521094000_rename_audit_functions_to_ph_os/migration.sql`, and three read-only subagent reports for rate-limit, bulk-export, and migration coverage
- files changed: `src/lib/api/rate-limit.ts`, `src/lib/api/rate-limit.test.ts`, `src/proxy.ts`, `src/proxy.test.ts`, `src/server/services/pdf-bulk-export.ts`, `src/server/services/pdf-bulk-export.test.ts`, `src/app/api/patients/medications/bulk-export/route.ts`, `src/app/api/patients/medications/bulk-export/route.test.ts`, `src/server/jobs/pdf-bulk-export.ts`, `src/app/api/jobs/[jobType]/route.ts`, `src/app/api/jobs/[jobType]/route.test.ts`, `tools/scripts/verify-ph-os-audit-migration.ts`, `package.json`, `.github/workflows/ci.yml`, `.codex/ralph-state.md`
- bugs found: DynamoDB rate limiting failed open to per-process memory in production and also used import-time cleanup timers unsafe for Edge proxy; NextAuth credentials login used generic write limits instead of strict auth limits; medication history bulk export queue audit was fire-and-forget after job creation and could leave export jobs without audit records; authenticated admins could trigger the bulk-export drain across all organizations; PH-OS audit function migration was not exercised by CI because E2E DB preparation used `prisma db push` instead of running migrations
- security risks found: reduced rate-limit bypass and Edge DoS risk by replacing import-time cleanup with request-time cleanup, failing closed when production distributed rate limiting is unavailable, and routing credentials login attempts through the strict auth limiter; reduced bulk-export audit gaps by writing the export audit inside the queue transaction; reduced cross-tenant side effects by scoping authenticated bulk-export drains to the caller's org while keeping API-key drains global; added a migration CI gate that runs `prisma migrate deploy` and verifies `ph_os_*` audit functions/triggers plus representative Task DML audit behavior
- performance issues found: request-time memory rate-limit cleanup replaces a top-level interval and avoids Edge import side effects; large bulk-export ZIP memory pressure remains a known unfixed issue that requires streaming ZIP/storage design
- validation commands: targeted Vitest for rate-limit, proxy, pdf-bulk-export service, bulk-export route, and job route; targeted ESLint for changed runtime files and verifier script; `pnpm --config.verify-deps-before-run=false exec tsc --noEmit --pretty false`; `actionlint .github/workflows/ci.yml`; `pnpm --config.verify-deps-before-run=false exec prisma validate --schema=prisma/schema/`; full `pnpm --config.verify-deps-before-run=false lint`; full `pnpm --config.verify-deps-before-run=false test`; full `pnpm --config.verify-deps-before-run=false build`; `pnpm --config.verify-deps-before-run=false audit --prod --audit-level moderate`; `git diff --check`; local `prisma migrate status` probe against `localhost:5433`
- validation results: targeted Vitest passed with 5 files / 41 tests after the final proxy/rate-limit changes; targeted ESLint passed; TypeScript passed; actionlint passed; Prisma schema validate passed; full ESLint passed; full Vitest passed with 526 files / 2487 tests; production build passed on Next 16.2.6 with 216 app routes; production dependency audit reported no known vulnerabilities; whitespace check passed; local migration status probe could not connect because no PostgreSQL server was running on `localhost:5433`

### 20260521-120658

- current task: fix bulk-export final artifact audit and add a bounded memory guard before synchronous ZIP generation
- files inspected: `git status --short`, `.codex/ralph-state.md`, `src/server/services/pdf-bulk-export.ts`, `src/server/services/pdf-bulk-export.test.ts`, `src/server/services/export-audit.ts`, `src/server/services/export-audit.test.ts`, `src/server/services/file-storage.ts`, and two read-only subagent reports for final audit and memory guard placement
- files changed: `src/server/services/pdf-bulk-export.ts`, `src/server/services/pdf-bulk-export.test.ts`, `.codex/ralph-state.md`
- bugs found: successful medication-history bulk export jobs wrote only the request-time export audit and did not audit the actual ZIP artifact, file ID, success count, or failed count; partial-success jobs had no audit record showing the actual successful PDF count; final audit failures could otherwise become completed jobs without an artifact audit if not coupled to the completion update; `zipSync` could run after rendering a very large set of PDFs with no total byte guard, causing a large synchronous memory/CPU spike before storage
- security risks found: improved PHI export auditability by writing final artifact audit in the same DB transaction as the completed job update; final audit failure now prevents ready notification and leaves the job in failed handling rather than exposing an unaudited completed export
- performance issues found: added `MEDICATION_HISTORY_BULK_EXPORT_MAX_TOTAL_PDF_BYTES` with a 128 MiB default guard before `zipSync` and `storeGeneratedFile`, preventing the largest avoidable synchronous ZIP allocation for oversized rendered PDFs; render-phase memory pressure remains a larger streaming-design follow-up

### 20260521-122340

- current task: fix rate-limit production operability and health/readiness hardening findings from the latest subagent audit
- files inspected: `git status --short`, `.codex/ralph-state.md`, Next 16 proxy docs, `src/lib/api/rate-limit.ts`, `src/lib/api/rate-limit.test.ts`, `src/lib/api/request-ip.ts`, `src/lib/api/request-ip.test.ts`, `src/proxy.ts`, `src/proxy.test.ts`, `src/app/api/health/route.ts`, `src/app/api/health/route.test.ts`, `src/server/services/backup-monitor.ts`, `.env.example`, and three read-only subagent reports for rate-limit security/test/correctness
- files changed: `src/lib/api/rate-limit.ts`, `src/lib/api/rate-limit.test.ts`, `src/lib/api/request-ip.ts`, `src/lib/api/request-ip.test.ts`, `src/proxy.ts`, `src/proxy.test.ts`, `src/app/api/health/route.ts`, `src/app/api/health/route.test.ts`, `.env.example`, `.codex/ralph-state.md`
- bugs found: production DynamoDB rate-limit calls could hang proxy execution with no timeout; quota exceeded, store unavailable, and store misconfiguration were all reported as indistinguishable 429s; `/api/health` could be blocked by the same fail-closed limiter it should help observe; production unauthenticated requests without a resolvable client IP collapsed into shared `ip:unknown` buckets; public health calls ran expensive backup/AWS diagnostics before admin authorization; deploy env docs did not mention required production rate-limit/DDB settings
- security risks found: reduced global lockout/bypass ambiguity by rejecting production unauthenticated API requests when no trusted client IP can be resolved, validating forwarded IP literals, supporting `TRUSTED_PROXY_HOPS`, preserving reason metadata for security events, and documenting production-only distributed rate-limit env requirements; public health no longer triggers backup/AWS diagnostics for anonymous callers
- performance issues found: added `RATE_LIMIT_DDB_TIMEOUT_MS` with a 1500ms default AbortController timeout so proxy rate-limit checks fail closed instead of hanging; split public health into cheap DB liveness while admin callers still receive backup diagnostics
- validation commands: targeted Vitest for rate-limit/request-ip/proxy/health; targeted ESLint for changed rate-limit/request-ip/proxy/health files; `pnpm --config.verify-deps-before-run=false exec tsc --noEmit --pretty false`; related auth/external-access Vitest; full `pnpm --config.verify-deps-before-run=false lint`; full `pnpm --config.verify-deps-before-run=false test`; full `pnpm --config.verify-deps-before-run=false build`; `pnpm --config.verify-deps-before-run=false audit --prod --audit-level moderate`; `git diff --check`

- security risks found: reduced static-key drift risk by preferring container runtime credentials over static env keys; kept production fail-closed for missing distributed credential sources; added table/IAM/KMS/TTL contract artifact and static tests; added WAF URI path/query redaction and disabled sampled requests for the Web ACL/API rate rule; added deployment verifier and runbook so misconfigured table/IAM/TTL can be caught before live traffic returns `RATE_LIMIT_UNAVAILABLE`; made `.env.example` trackable and documented runtime-role preference
- performance issues found: no request-path performance change beyond optional one-time container credential lookup/cache; verifier and runbook are deploy-time only
- validation commands: targeted Vitest for `src/lib/api/rate-limit.test.ts` and `src/proxy.test.ts`; JSON parse validation for `tools/infra/rate-limit-dynamodb.json`, `tools/infra/aws-waf-web-acl.json`, and `tools/infra/cloudwatch-alarms.json`; targeted ESLint for rate-limit/proxy/verifier files; `pnpm --config.verify-deps-before-run=false exec tsc --noEmit --pretty false`; `pnpm --config.verify-deps-before-run=false exec prisma validate --schema=prisma/schema/`; `git diff --check`; full `pnpm --config.verify-deps-before-run=false lint`; full `pnpm --config.verify-deps-before-run=false test`; full `pnpm --config.verify-deps-before-run=false build`; `pnpm --config.verify-deps-before-run=false audit --prod --audit-level moderate`

### 20260521-140153

- current task: remove notification SSE proxy rate-limit bypass and harden SSE connection cleanup from subagent findings
- files inspected: `git status --short`, `.codex/ralph-state.md`, `src/proxy.ts`, `src/proxy.test.ts`, `src/app/api/notifications/stream/route.ts`, `src/app/api/notifications/stream/route.test.ts`, `src/lib/api/rate-limit.ts`, `src/lib/api/rate-limit.test.ts`, `src/server/adapters/realtime/redis-adapter.ts`, notification/realtime client call sites referenced by subagents, and read-only security/performance/test/reviewer subagent reports for SSE rate-limit and lifecycle behavior

- bugs found: `GET /api/notifications/stream` bypassed the distributed proxy rate limiter entirely and relied only on process-local SSE connection gauges; SSE route tests did not cover connection-cap 429, release-on-abort/cancel, or gauge semantics; the stream route could leave realtime listeners/counters active when a request was aborted during subscription, when a stream body was cancelled, or when Redis registered a listener before subscribe failed
- security risks found: reduced availability abuse by making stream opens consume the normal read rate budget before route auth/realtime work; kept `/api/health` as the only rate-limit-exempt API path; preserved route auth and per-user SSE cap; residual org-wide realtime metadata exposure remains unfixed because removing or redacting the org channel affects realtime workflow API/UI semantics
- performance issues found: reduced unauthenticated/over-budget stream-open amplification at the proxy; reduced listener/counter leaks by adding idempotent teardown, abort listener removal, `ReadableStream.cancel()` cleanup, and tracked/idempotent subscription cleanup for late success or partial failure
- validation commands: targeted `pnpm --config.verify-deps-before-run=false exec vitest run src/proxy.test.ts src/app/api/notifications/stream/route.test.ts src/lib/api/rate-limit.test.ts`; targeted ESLint for proxy/stream/rate-limit files; `pnpm --config.verify-deps-before-run=false exec tsc --noEmit --pretty false`; full `pnpm --config.verify-deps-before-run=false lint`; `git diff --check`; full `pnpm --config.verify-deps-before-run=false test`; full `pnpm --config.verify-deps-before-run=false build`; `pnpm --config.verify-deps-before-run=false audit --prod --audit-level moderate`; `pnpm --config.verify-deps-before-run=false exec prisma validate --schema=prisma/schema/`
- validation results: targeted Vitest passed with 3 files / 59 tests; targeted ESLint passed; TypeScript passed; full ESLint passed; whitespace check passed; full Vitest passed with 527 files / 2554 tests; production build passed on Next 16.2.6 with 216 app routes; production dependency audit reported no known vulnerabilities; Prisma schema validate passed
- remaining work: broader PH-OS Bug Ledger remains active. Remaining concrete follow-ups include org-wide realtime payload scoping/redaction for PHI-adjacent metadata, true distributed active SSE connection leases or a dedicated stream-open attempt limiter, client-side singleton realtime stream to reduce per-tab duplicate connections, reconnect jitter/backoff for 5-minute stream lifetime waves, true streaming ZIP/S3 upload design, backup-monitor production skipped-check semantics, optional generated-file metadata table migration, and further admin diagnostics endpoint design if per-patient bulk-export failure details are required

### 20260521-151704

- current task: close high-confidence presence authz/channel boundary issues without moving presence to org-wide realtime
- files inspected: `git status --short`, `.codex/ralph-state.md`, `src/app/api/presence/route.ts`, `src/app/api/presence/route.test.ts`, `src/server/services/presence-store.ts`, `src/server/services/org-realtime-policy.test.ts`, `src/app/api/notifications/stream/route.ts`, `src/lib/hooks/use-collaborative-form.ts`, `src/components/features/collaboration/presence-avatars.tsx`, `src/lib/auth/context.ts`, `src/lib/auth/permissions.ts`, `src/lib/auth/middleware.ts`, `src/lib/auth/visit-schedule-access.ts`, `src/server/services/prescription-access.ts`, `src/app/api/dispense-tasks/[id]/route.ts`, and read-only security/test subagent reports for presence authz/realtime gaps
- files changed: `src/app/api/presence/route.ts`, `src/app/api/presence/route.test.ts`, `src/server/services/org-realtime-policy.test.ts`, `.codex/ralph-state.md`
- bugs found: `/api/presence` accepted arbitrary `entity_type`/`entity_id` behind only `canDispense`, so same-org users could write/read presence for unassigned or unsupported entities before any entity-level access check; presence realtime channel omitted `orgId`, so identical entity ids in different organizations could collide on the adapter channel; presence heartbeat awaited realtime broadcast directly, so a Redis/realtime outage could turn best-effort presence updates into failed POSTs after the in-memory presence write
- security risks found: reduced same-org-but-unassigned PHI metadata exposure by allowlisting presence entities to `dispense_task` and `visit_record`, checking org plus assignment-scoped access with existing medication-cycle and visit-schedule helpers before store reads/writes/broadcasts, returning before side effects on unsupported/inaccessible entities, and keeping presence off org-wide realtime; reduced cross-tenant channel collision risk with `presence:${orgId}:${entity_type}:${entity_id}` while preserving policy tests that forbid org-wide presence publishing

### 20260521-170556

- current task: fix Yjs collaboration room token client renewal and fail-closed handling for authorization loss
- files inspected: `git status --short`, `.codex/ralph-state.md`, `src/lib/hooks/use-collaborative-form.ts`, `src/lib/hooks/use-collaborative-form.test.tsx`, `src/lib/collaboration/yjs-provider.ts`, `src/lib/collaboration/form-yjs-bridge.ts`, `src/app/api/collaboration/room-token/route.ts`, `src/app/api/collaboration/room-token/route.test.ts`, `src/server/services/collaboration-room-token.ts`, `src/lib/api/response.ts`, y-websocket provider implementation/types, and read-only reviewer/test/security subagent reports for collaboration token lifecycle
- files changed: `src/lib/hooks/use-collaborative-form.ts`, `src/lib/hooks/use-collaborative-form.test.tsx`, `src/app/api/collaboration/room-token/route.ts`, `src/app/api/collaboration/room-token/route.test.ts`, `.codex/ralph-state.md`
- bugs found: client fetched a 5-minute collaboration room token once and never renewed it; reconnects after token expiry would keep using stale provider credentials; token renewal denial was initially treated like a transient retry, leaving the old provider alive until server-side expiry; room-token API returned a secret-bearing JSON response without explicit no-store headers
- security risks found: reduced stale authorization exposure for honest clients by destroying the active provider on 4xx renewal denial and setting awareness/connected state to disconnected; kept 429/5xx/network failures as transient retry without immediately dropping an otherwise still-valid provider; added no-store/no-cache headers to the room-token response
- performance issues found: token refresh scheduling is one timer per active collaborative form, with a 60-second refresh skew and 5-second retry floor; no unbounded loop introduced

### 20260521-175351

- current task: harden collaboration room-token transient retry behavior with `Retry-After`, bounded exponential backoff, and deterministic regression coverage
- files inspected: `git status --short`, `.codex/ralph-state.md`, `src/lib/hooks/use-collaborative-form.ts`, `src/lib/hooks/use-collaborative-form.test.tsx`, `src/app/api/collaboration/room-token/route.ts`, `src/app/api/collaboration/room-token/route.test.ts`, `src/server/services/collaboration-room-token.ts`, and read-only reviewer/test/security subagent reports for room-token retry/backoff behavior
- files changed: `src/lib/hooks/use-collaborative-form.ts`, `src/lib/hooks/use-collaborative-form.test.tsx`, `src/app/api/collaboration/room-token/route.ts`, `src/app/api/collaboration/room-token/route.test.ts`, `.codex/ralph-state.md`
- bugs found: 429/5xx/network token fetch failures retried on a fixed 5-second interval; 429/503 `Retry-After` headers were ignored; collaboration token unavailable 503 responses did not tell clients how long to back off; several hook tests used absolute token expiry timestamps that would become expired as wall-clock time advanced
- security risks found: reduced endpoint pressure and retry amplification against the room-token/rate-limit path by respecting `Retry-After` and adding bounded exponential backoff with jitter; kept non-429 4xx responses terminal so authorization denials still fail closed without retrying or preserving presence side effects
- performance issues found: reduced synchronized retry storms by replacing fixed 5-second transient retries with 5s/10s/... bounded exponential delays plus jitter, and by adding `Retry-After: 60` to known server-side token-unavailable 503 responses
- validation commands: Prettier on changed hook/route files; targeted Vitest for hook/provider/route tests; related collaboration Vitest for hook/provider/route/token/access/realtime/textarea tests; targeted ESLint for changed hook/route files; `pnpm --config.verify-deps-before-run=false exec tsc --noEmit --pretty false`; targeted `git diff --check`; full `pnpm --config.verify-deps-before-run=false test`; full `pnpm --config.verify-deps-before-run=false lint`; full `pnpm --config.verify-deps-before-run=false build`; `pnpm --config.verify-deps-before-run=false audit --audit-level high`
- validation results: targeted hook/provider/route Vitest passed with 3 files / 22 tests; related collaboration suite passed with 8 files / 81 tests; targeted ESLint passed; TypeScript passed; targeted whitespace check passed; full Vitest passed with 543 files / 2708 tests; full ESLint passed; production build passed on Next 16.2.6 with 217 app routes; dependency audit reported no known vulnerabilities
- remaining work: broader PH-OS Bug Ledger remains active. - performance issues found: no product runtime change; browser validation now waits on the precise patient search API response instead of broad sleeps, and the larger test timeout only covers local dev-server compile/restart overhead

### 20260531-130900

- current task: prevent facility visit context decode recursion and guard malformed grouped-visit context JSON
- files inspected: `.codex/ralph-state.md`, `src/server/services/collaboration-room-token.ts`, `src/server/services/collaboration-room-token.test.ts`, `src/app/api/collaboration/room-token/route.ts`, `src/app/api/collaboration/room-token/route.test.ts`, `src/lib/visits/facility-visit-context.ts`, `src/lib/visits/facility-visit-context.test.ts`, `src/lib/db/json.ts`

- bugs found: `decodeFacilityVisitContext` retried `decodeURIComponent(raw)` recursively on any parse failure; for invalid non-encoded strings, the decoded value equals the original, causing unbounded recursion instead of returning null. - performance issues found: fixed an unbounded recursion path on malformed input; normal successful decode remains a single JSON parse plus bounded patient normalization

### 20260531-131848

- current task: harden offline sync queue payload decoding before network sync and conflict handling
- files inspected: `.codex/ralph-state.md`, `src/lib/stores/sync-engine.ts`, `src/lib/stores/sync-engine.test.ts`, `src/lib/db/json.ts`, `src/lib/stores/offline-db.ts`, sync engine call sites in visit record and schedule views
- files changed: `src/lib/stores/sync-engine.ts`, `src/lib/stores/sync-engine.test.ts`, `.codex/ralph-state.md`
- bugs found: `processSyncQueue()` used `(await decryptOfflinePayload(item.payload)) ?? item.payload` directly as the POST body, so malformed encrypted queue payloads or legacy non-object strings could be sent to API routes instead of being contained to the queue item. Conflict handling also reparsed the decrypted payload with a loose generic cast when building the local conflict snapshot
- security risks found: offline sync payloads now must decode to a JSON object before being sent to server endpoints or used in conflict overwrite flows. Invalid payloads are marked failed with `Invalid sync payload`, conflict metadata is cleared, and no malformed body is sent over the network. No auth/authz behavior changed
- performance issues found: no meaningful runtime performance issue found; validation adds one bounded JSON parse/object check per queued item before existing network I/O

### 20260531-135955

- current task: harden Cognito ID token payload decoding before authenticated user fields are derived
- files inspected: `.codex/ralph-state.md`, `src/server/services/cognito-auth.ts`, Cognito auth route/test references found by `rg`, `src/lib/db/json.ts`, `src/lib/auth/config.ts`
- files changed: `src/server/services/cognito-auth.ts`, `src/server/services/cognito-auth.test.ts`, `.codex/ralph-state.md`
- bugs found: Cognito ID token payload decode used `JSON.parse(Buffer.from(...)) as { sub?: string; email?: string; name?: string }`, so malformed JSON could leak raw parse errors and array/scalar payloads could pass through type assertions before authenticated user fields were derived
- security risks found: Cognito ID token payloads now fail closed unless the payload decodes to a JSON object. Invalid/missing payloads, malformed JSON, arrays, nulls, and scalar payloads consistently throw `COGNITO_ID_TOKEN_INVALID`; optional `sub`, `email`, and `name` claims are accepted only when string-typed. ### 20260531-140522

- current task: harden MFA recovery-code persisted setting parsing before recovery flow mutation
- files inspected: `.codex/ralph-state.md`, `src/server/services/mfa-recovery.ts`, `src/server/services/mfa-recovery.test.ts`, `src/app/api/auth/mfa/recovery/route.ts`, MFA recovery route tests found by `rg`, `src/lib/db/json.ts`
- files changed: `src/server/services/mfa-recovery.ts`, `src/server/services/mfa-recovery.test.ts`, `.codex/ralph-state.md`
- bugs found: persisted MFA recovery-code settings accepted mixed-type `hashes` by silently filtering non-string values and accepted missing `generatedAt` by replacing it with the current time. - security risks found: recovery-code settings now fail closed unless the top-level value is a JSON object, `version` is 1, every hash is a string, and `generatedAt` is a string. Malformed arrays, mixed hash arrays, and missing timestamps are rejected without updating or deleting the stored setting. No hashing algorithm, transaction isolation, or recovery-code issuance behavior changed
- performance issues found: no meaningful performance issue found; parsing remains bounded to one persisted setting row and one in-memory hash array scan

- bugs found: DynamoDB rate-limit code parsed container credentials and DynamoDB `UpdateItem` counters with type assertions. Malformed container credential fields could reach SigV4 signing, and malformed `hit_count`/`reset_at` values could become `NaN` in quota results instead of failing the distributed store path cleanly. TypeScript also caught that optional container `Token` parsing could pass `null` into `AwsCredentials.sessionToken`

DynamoDB update responses now fail closed unless `Attributes.hit_count.N` and `Attributes.reset_at.N` are finite numeric strings. In production, malformed credentials/counters now produce `store_unavailable` rather than allowing malformed quota state. No credential URL allowlist, request signing, static credential fallback ordering, production deny-all guard, route canonicalization behavior, or DynamoDB request body changed in this slice

Malformed successful overview responses now become a typed `PatientMcsOverviewQueryError` instead of propagating arbitrary response data into patient-facing UI. - security risks found: the route now exports `DrugPackageInsertResponse` and returns an object that satisfies that response contract, with package-insert and version-history dates explicitly serialized to ISO strings before `success()`. Tests now use a shared `readPackageInsertPayload()` helper and `requirePackageInsert()` guard before reading nested sections. Existing dirty JSON hardening for section formatting and malformed alert-rule conditions was preserved and revalidated. No auth permission, route params, database query scope, rule matching semantics, or interaction merging behavior was intentionally changed in this slice
- performance issues found: no meaningful runtime performance issue found; response assembly still maps the same package inserts/interactions/rules once. Test-side validation is bounded to one response payload per case
- validation commands: `pnpm exec prettier --write 'src/app/api/drug-masters/[id]/package-insert/route.ts' 'src/app/api/drug-masters/[id]/package-insert/route.test.ts'`; `pnpm --config.verify-deps-before-run=false exec vitest run 'src/app/api/drug-masters/[id]/package-insert/route.test.ts'`; `pnpm --config.verify-deps-before-run=false exec eslint 'src/app/api/drug-masters/[id]/package-insert/route.ts' 'src/app/api/drug-masters/[id]/package-insert/route.test.ts' --max-warnings=0`; `pnpm --config.verify-deps-before-run=false exec tsc --noEmit --pretty false`; `rg -n "DrugPackageInsertResponse|DrugPackageInsertSections|latestPackageInsert|readPackageInsertPayload|requirePackageInsert|alertRuleIds|await response\\.json\\(\\)|\\{ id: string \\}|applicable_alert_rules\\.map" 'src/app/api/drug-masters/[id]/package-insert/route.ts' 'src/app/api/drug-masters/[id]/package-insert/route.test.ts'`; `git diff --check -- 'src/app/api/drug-masters/[id]/package-insert/route.ts' 'src/app/api/drug-masters/[id]/package-insert/route.test.ts'`

### 20260531-220237

- current task: harden DynamoDB rate-limit credential and counter response parsing before auth/API gates accept malformed distributed-store state
- files inspected: `.codex/ralph-state.md`, `package.json`, `src/lib/api/rate-limit.ts`, `src/lib/api/rate-limit.test.ts`, `src/lib/db/json.ts`, current git status, JSON/response-boundary candidate search output, read-only subagent mapping for next candidates
- files changed: `src/lib/api/rate-limit.ts`, `src/lib/api/rate-limit.test.ts`, `.codex/ralph-state.md`
- bugs found: the ECS/container credentials parser accepted whitespace-padded required credential fields and treated missing or invalid `Expiration` as a null expiry, which could cache malformed container credentials indefinitely. The DynamoDB rate-limit response parser also accepted finite but invalid counter values such as negative `hit_count` or non-positive `reset_at`, which could turn a malformed distributed-store response into misleading quota state
- security risks found: DynamoDB-backed rate limiting now fails closed unless container credentials include trim-normalized nonblank access key / secret key values and a valid expiration timestamp. DynamoDB `hit_count` and `reset_at` now must be positive safe integers before they influence allow/deny decisions. No route catalog, path canonicalization, credential URL allowlist, SigV4 signing, production deny-all fallback, in-memory fallback policy, or API rate-limit thresholds changed
- performance issues found: no meaningful performance issue found; validation adds small string trims and integer checks around already materialized JSON responses
- validation commands: `pnpm exec prettier --write src/lib/api/rate-limit.ts src/lib/api/rate-limit.test.ts`; `pnpm --config.verify-deps-before-run=false exec vitest run src/lib/api/rate-limit.test.ts`; `pnpm --config.verify-deps-before-run=false exec eslint src/lib/api/rate-limit.ts src/lib/api/rate-limit.test.ts src/lib/db/json.ts --max-warnings=0`; `pnpm --config.verify-deps-before-run=false exec tsc --noEmit --pretty false`; `rg -n "parseRequiredTrimmedString|parseOptionalTrimmedString|parseDynamoPositiveIntegerAttribute|invalid expiration|non-positive counters|Date\\.parse\\(expiration\\)|Number\\.isSafeInteger" src/lib/api/rate-limit.ts src/lib/api/rate-limit.test.ts`; `git diff --check -- src/lib/api/rate-limit.ts src/lib/api/rate-limit.test.ts .codex/ralph-state.md`; `git status --short -- src/lib/api/rate-limit.ts src/lib/api/rate-limit.test.ts .codex/ralph-state.md`
- validation results: formatting passed; focused Vitest passed with 1 file / 27 tests; targeted ESLint passed with zero warnings; TypeScript passed; grep confirmed the stricter container credential string/expiration parsing, positive integer DynamoDB parser, and new invalid-expiration/non-positive-counter coverage are present; whitespace check passed
- remaining work: broad refactoring/stability goal remains active. Existing dirty files and unrelated prior modifications remain. Full repo validation, distributed DynamoDB integration proof, and broader API/client response-boundary hardening were not run in this focused rate-limit slice

- bugs found: push subscription create/delete accepted valid URLs and key strings without trimming, so leading/trailing whitespace could become part of the unique endpoint key and later prevent unsubscribe matching. Subscription keys used `min(1)` without trim, so whitespace-only `p256dh` or `auth` values could be persisted. The route also accepted non-HTTPS endpoint URLs even though browser push endpoints should be secure URLs
- security risks found: subscribe/unsubscribe payloads now fail closed unless the body is a JSON object with a trim-normalized HTTPS endpoint. Push keys now trim and reject blank values before DB upsert. - bugs found: available-shifts GET accepted invalid calendar dates and malformed time strings before Prisma lookup. When `time_from` and `time_to` were both provided, the route spread two `OR` properties into the same Prisma `where`, so the later `time_to` condition overwrote the `time_from` bound and could return shifts that did not cover the requested start time
- security risks found: available-shifts query input now uses the shared pharmacist-shift date/time validation before any shift or holiday query. Dates must be real `YYYY-MM-DD` keys, optional times are trim-normalized HH:mm/HH:mm:ss values, and reversed time windows fail before DB access. Auth permission, org scoping, holiday closure filtering, response envelope, and included user fields were not relaxed
- performance issues found: malformed date/time queries now fail before `pharmacistShift.findMany` or `businessHoliday.findMany`. Valid time-window queries keep both bounds in an `AND` array instead of accidentally dropping one predicate. Validation is bounded to a few string trims, regex checks, and one date-order comparison

- bugs found: `/api/files/complete` accepted only exact UUID text, so padded `file_id` values failed instead of normalizing like the presign route, and blank `etag` strings were passed into service completion instead of being treated as absent. `completeUploadedFile()` also re-processed already uploaded records, which could overwrite `uploadedBy`, `etag`, and `completedAt` metadata on a retry or duplicate complete call after the file was already marked uploaded
- security risks found: completion requests now fail closed unless the root body is an object and `file_id` is a trim-normalized UUID; blank ETags normalize to absent and ETags are bounded before service use. Completed file records still pass the same stored-file access checks, but retries now return existing metadata without S3 HEAD or metadata update, preventing authorized duplicate complete calls from rewriting upload attribution/timestamps. Auth, org scoping, stored-file access checks, S3 HEAD validation for pending uploads, object size/content-type checks, ETag mismatch handling, and response envelope were not relaxed
- performance issues found: malformed blank file IDs now fail before storage lookup, and already completed files no longer perform unnecessary S3 HEAD or settings update work on retry. Pending uploads still perform the same S3 validation before being marked uploaded
- validation commands: `npx vitest run src/app/api/files/complete/route.test.ts src/server/services/file-storage.test.ts`; `npx prettier --write src/app/api/files/complete/route.ts src/app/api/files/complete/route.test.ts src/server/services/file-storage.ts src/server/services/file-storage.test.ts`; `npx vitest run src/app/api/files/complete/route.test.ts src/app/api/files/presigned-upload/route.test.ts src/server/services/file-storage.test.ts`; `npx eslint src/app/api/files/complete/route.ts src/app/api/files/complete/route.test.ts src/app/api/files/presigned-upload/route.ts src/app/api/files/presigned-upload/route.test.ts src/server/services/file-storage.ts src/server/services/file-storage.test.ts`; `npx tsc --noEmit`; `git diff --check -- src/app/api/files/complete/route.ts src/app/api/files/complete/route.test.ts src/server/services/file-storage.ts src/server/services/file-storage.test.ts`; `rg -n "trimStringOrUndefined|file_id: z\\.string\\(\\)\\.trim|blank etags|rejects blank file ids|keeps completed file metadata unchanged|record\\.status === 'uploaded'|completeUploadedFileMock" src/app/api/files/complete/route.ts src/app/api/files/complete/route.test.ts src/server/services/file-storage.ts src/server/services/file-storage.test.ts`; `git status --short -- .codex/ralph-state.md src/app/api/files/complete/route.ts src/app/api/files/complete/route.test.ts src/server/services/file-storage.ts src/server/services/file-storage.test.ts src/app/api/files/presigned-upload/route.ts src/app/api/files/presigned-upload/route.test.ts`
- validation results: focused complete/storage Vitest passed with 2 files / 45 tests; file upload lifecycle Vitest passed with 3 files / 61 tests; Prettier reported all touched files unchanged; targeted ESLint passed with zero warnings; TypeScript passed; whitespace check passed; grep confirmed the complete-route trim/blank ETag handling, blank file ID rejection, completed-file retry preservation, and no-update/no-S3 retry coverage are present. `git status --short` confirms the repo still has unrelated dirty tracked/untracked files plus the current focused changes
- remaining work: broad refactoring/stability goal remains active. Existing dirty files and unrelated prior modifications remain. Full repo validation, live S3/browser upload proof, file download-route input normalization, row-level staff import schema errors, Cognito compensation planning, and broader phone validation parity remain outside this focused file-completion slice

- security risks found: both download routes now fail closed on blank file IDs before stored-file metadata lookup. Nonblank IDs are trim-normalized before org-scoped storage access checks and redirect/signature generation. The redirect flag is trim-normalized without broadening accepted values beyond `1`. Auth context, org scoping, stored-file access checks, no-store headers, presigned URL generation, and response envelopes were not relaxed
- performance issues found: blank file IDs now stop before settings lookup, stored-file access checks, and presigned URL work. Padded file IDs avoid avoidable failed lookups against unnormalized keys. Validation is a bounded trim check over one route param

- bugs found: patient care-team, conditions, contacts, labs create/update, insurance create/update, visit constraints, packaging, MCS sync, e-prescription intake, and medication-history bulk export write routes still used local `req.json().catch` plus `readJsonObject` request-body parsing while adjacent API clusters had moved to the shared invalid-body helper. Several routes only covered non-object payloads, so malformed JSON parse failures were not consistently locked against patient lookup, assignment-scope checks, visit-record provenance checks, external adapter calls, transactions, queueing, or create/update side effects
- security risks found: malformed or non-object JSON bodies now fail closed through `readJsonObjectRequestBody` before patient/case/insurance/lab lookup, assignment-scope checks, external-professional validation, visit-record provenance validation, contact/condition/care-team replacement, insurance/lab/packaging/visit-constraint writes, sensitive MCS sync, e-prescription adapter calls, prescription intake creation, medication-history export queueing, or background queue draining. Existing auth gates, org scoping, patient assignment filters, privacy masking, sensitive-data role checks, provenance validation, and response envelopes were not relaxed
- performance issues found: malformed JSON now stops through one bounded parse/catch and object-shape helper path before DB lookups, transactions, external adapter calls, provenance checks, queue creation, background drain scheduling, or create/update writes. The change removes repeated request-boundary code across twelve patient-facing write routes

Existing untracked `src/lib/api/request-body.ts` and `src/lib/api/request-body.test.ts` were reused and validated but not rewritten in this slice
- bugs found: nine pharmacy drug stock/request/template write routes still used local `req.json().catch` or direct `readJsonObject` request-body parsing, so malformed JSON was not locked against site/drug/stock/request/template lookups, transactions, stock upsert/import/copy/review/follow-up work, request create/decision, template create/apply, or audit writes. Medical safety review also found persisted `requested_payload` approval replay could turn corrupted/legacy JSON into default stock changes, and template apply could silently drop malformed rows or apply orphaned drug/preferred-generic references
- security risks found: malformed/non-object incoming JSON now fails closed through `readJsonObjectRequestBody` before the targeted route-local DB lookup, mutation, transaction, or audit paths. Stock request creation and approval now use a shared strict pharmacy stock payload schema that rejects blank preferred generic IDs and revalidates referenced preferred generics before approval replay. Template apply now rejects malformed persisted rows and missing/invalid drug references before non-dry-run stock lookup/mutation/audit, while dry-run exposes invalid counts/details without applying changes. Existing canAdmin gates, org/site scoping, preferred generic existence/type/same-generic-name checks, and response envelopes were not relaxed
- performance issues found: malformed incoming JSON and corrupted persisted formulary payload/template data now stop through bounded parse/schema/reference validation before expensive DB lookups, bulk import/copy/review/follow-up operations, transaction callbacks, stock upserts, template application loops, request status updates, or audit writes. Repeated request-body parsing logic was removed from nine route handlers
- validation commands: `pnpm --config.verify-deps-before-run=false exec prettier --write ...target pharmacy drug stock/request/template route and test files...`; `pnpm --config.verify-deps-before-run=false exec vitest run src/lib/api/request-body.test.ts src/app/api/pharmacy-drug-stocks/route.test.ts src/app/api/pharmacy-drug-stocks/bulk/route.test.ts src/app/api/pharmacy-drug-stocks/copy/route.test.ts src/app/api/pharmacy-drug-stocks/review/route.test.ts src/app/api/pharmacy-drug-stocks/safety-follow-up/route.test.ts src/app/api/pharmacy-drug-stock-requests/route.test.ts 'src/app/api/pharmacy-drug-stock-requests/[id]/route.test.ts' src/app/api/pharmacy-drug-stock-templates/route.test.ts 'src/app/api/pharmacy-drug-stock-templates/[id]/apply/route.test.ts'`; `pnpm --config.verify-deps-before-run=false exec eslint src/lib/api/request-body.ts src/lib/api/request-body.test.ts src/lib/validations/pharmacy-drug-stock.ts ...target pharmacy drug stock/request/template route and test files... --max-warnings=0`; `pnpm --config.verify-deps-before-run=false exec tsc --noEmit --pretty false`; `git diff --check -- .codex/ralph-state.md src/lib/api/request-body.ts src/lib/api/request-body.test.ts src/lib/validations/pharmacy-drug-stock.ts ...target pharmacy drug stock/request/template route and test files...`; focused `rg` for remaining raw request-body parsing, helper usage, malformed JSON coverage, strict stock payload schema usage, corrupted approval/template messages, invalid item counts, and missing reference coverage
- validation results: initial focused Vitest passed with 10 files / 57 tests after the request-body migration; medical safety reviewers found persisted formulary JSON mutation risks, which were fixed and retested. A temporary `ReferenceError: sourceItemCount is not defined` in template preview was caught by focused Vitest and fixed. ### 20260601-033352

- current task: apply shared request-body parsing to external-access grant/self-report write boundaries and reduce OTP exposure in external sharing responses
- files inspected: `git status --short`, `.codex/ralph-state.md`, memory registry pointers for CareViaX continuation, Vercel Next.js skill guidance, `node_modules/next/dist/docs/01-app/03-api-reference/03-file-conventions/route.md`, `src/lib/api/request-body.ts`, `src/lib/api/request-body.test.ts`, `src/lib/db/json.ts`, `src/app/api/external-access/route.ts`, `src/app/api/external-access/route.test.ts`, `src/app/api/external-access/[token]/self-report/route.ts`, `src/app/api/external-access/[token]/self-report/route.test.ts`, `src/server/services/external-access.ts`, mapper/verifier/privacy reviewer outputs, focused diffs, and focused grep results for raw request-body parsing/helper usage/malformed JSON and OTP coverage
- files changed: `src/app/api/external-access/route.ts`, `src/app/api/external-access/route.test.ts`, `src/app/api/external-access/[token]/self-report/route.ts`, `src/app/api/external-access/[token]/self-report/route.test.ts`, `.codex/ralph-state.md`. Existing untracked `src/lib/api/request-body.ts` and `src/lib/api/request-body.test.ts` were reused and validated but not rewritten in this slice. The target files already contained unrelated dirty trimming, scope, and non-object payload coverage before this pass; this slice preserved that work
- bugs found: external-access grant create and external token self-report create still used local `req.json().catch` or direct body parsing, so malformed JSON route behavior was not locked against scope validation, org-reference validation, patient/case lookup, grant create/update, token issuance, SMS send, external grant validation, patient self-report creation, or communication-event creation. Privacy review also found body `otp` could be silently stripped by non-strict Zod when a valid `x-otp` header was present, and SMS-delivered grants still returned raw OTP in the API response
- security risks found: malformed/non-object JSON now fails closed through `readJsonObjectRequestBody` before the targeted route-local sensitive validation, DB lookup, mutation, token, SMS, or self-report/event write paths. Self-report now rejects a body `otp` key before `validateExternalAccessGrant`, even when `x-otp` is present, preserving the header-only secret design. SMS-delivered external grants no longer include raw `otp` in the response; manual-delivery fallback still includes it for operator delivery and remains a low-risk operational exposure requiring response-body log redaction/telemetry controls. Existing `canReport` auth, self-report rate limiting, org scoping, assignment checks, OTP header usage, token hashing, manual fallback, and response envelopes were not relaxed
- performance issues found: malformed incoming JSON now stops through one bounded parse/catch and object-shape helper path before scope validation, org-reference checks, patient/case lookup, transaction callbacks, token issuance, SMS adapter work, external grant validation, or self-report/communication-event writes. SMS successful responses also avoid sending an unnecessary secret payload back to the authenticated management client
- validation commands: `pnpm --config.verify-deps-before-run=false exec prettier --write src/app/api/external-access/route.ts src/app/api/external-access/route.test.ts 'src/app/api/external-access/[token]/self-report/route.ts' 'src/app/api/external-access/[token]/self-report/route.test.ts'`; `pnpm --config.verify-deps-before-run=false exec vitest run src/lib/api/request-body.test.ts src/app/api/external-access/route.test.ts 'src/app/api/external-access/[token]/self-report/route.test.ts'`; `pnpm --config.verify-deps-before-run=false exec eslint src/lib/api/request-body.ts src/lib/api/request-body.test.ts src/app/api/external-access/route.ts src/app/api/external-access/route.test.ts 'src/app/api/external-access/[token]/self-report/route.ts' 'src/app/api/external-access/[token]/self-report/route.test.ts' --max-warnings=0`; `pnpm --config.verify-deps-before-run=false exec tsc --noEmit --pretty false`; `git diff --check -- .codex/ralph-state.md src/lib/api/request-body.ts src/lib/api/request-body.test.ts src/app/api/external-access/route.ts src/app/api/external-access/route.test.ts 'src/app/api/external-access/[token]/self-report/route.ts' 'src/app/api/external-access/[token]/self-report/route.test.ts'`; focused `rg` for remaining raw request-body parsing/helper usage and OTP behavior in `src/app/api/external-access`

Verifier recheck passed and confirmed helper usage, malformed/non-object fail-fast, body-OTP rejection before grant validation, SMS response OTP omission, manual response OTP presence, and no auth/rate-limit order regression. Existing auth, org scoping, offline PHI encryption, collaboration access-denied behavior, and workflow normalization checks were not relaxed
- performance issues found: no large performance issue. The change removes repeated async catch wrappers and keeps invalid response parsing on a single bounded helper path before downstream normalization, conflict encryption, provider creation, or reference-error response construction

- bugs found: consent record GET/PATCH/revoke and patient self-report GET/PATCH still had blank dynamic ID paths that could reach downstream lookups, request-body parsing, transaction callbacks, update/revoke branches, audit writes, task/grant side effects, or full PHI row hydration. Consent record GET/PATCH also lacked an explicit case-assignment access preflight before full record hydration/update. Consent revoke lacked a transaction-time assignment recheck before destructive side effects, over-revoked case-scoped external grants at patient scope, created unanchored workflow exceptions when no active medication cycle existed, and could select an unassigned fallback case for follow-up task context
- security risks found: reduced IDOR and stale-assignment risks by adding normalized blank-ID fail-closed paths, minimal preflight selects, case-scoped patient access checks before full consent hydration or mutation, transaction-time assignment rechecks before revoke side effects, guarded `updated_at` update/revoke writes, and case-scoped external grant revocation. Reduced PHI exposure by ensuring pre-assignment consent lookups select only access/concurrency fields and by avoiding unassigned fallback case linkage in revoke follow-up work
- performance issues found: blank IDs now stop before avoidable request-body parsing, full consent/self-report lookups, assignment expansion, transaction callbacks, update/revoke work, audit writes, task upserts, external-grant updates, and workflow exception processing. Consent preflights now avoid full row hydration until access passes
