Existing unrelated dirty files were preserved
- bugs found: QR draft GET/DELETE/confirm and prescription-intake QR fallback paths allowed blank route IDs, non-object/malformed JSON, stale pending draft claims, and intake side effects to progress too far. QR fallback via `/api/prescription-intakes` could confirm a QR draft into non-QR `source_type`, confirm drafts whose parsed patient identity was missing, and create intake records after a draft claim without a single transaction rollback path. QR draft duplicate detection was best-effort, patient-bound only, order-sensitive for multi-page payloads, and not backed by a DB uniqueness guard. Prescription intake date validation allowed invalid calendar dates, reversed line date ranges, future issue dates, and instant-based expiry drift
- security risks found: reduced wrong-patient and duplicate-dispensing risk by requiring route ID normalization, object JSON bodies, patient access checks, selected-patient identity lookup, QR parsed identity verification with unverifiable identity rejection, assignment-scoped draft lookup/claim, pending-only `updateMany` claim before intake creation, transaction rollback on intake failures, `qr_scan` source enforcement for `qr_draft_id`, canonical QR payload hashes, and a partial unique active payload-hash index. Response realtime events remain sanitized and existing auth/assignment/org scoping was not relaxed
- performance issues found: blank IDs, malformed/non-object bodies, future/expired prescriptions, invalid dates, identity mismatch/unverifiable QR data, non-QR QR fallback requests, duplicate QR hashes, processed drafts, and lost claims now stop before avoidable parser/mapper work where applicable, DB writes, supplemental record attachment, intake creation, webhook notification, realtime broadcast, operational task creation, and profile sync. QR duplicate lookup now uses an indexed `qr_payload_hash` instead of JSON equality for the active guard
- validation commands: `pnpm --config.verify-deps-before-run=false exec prettier --write ...target QR/prescription files...`; `pnpm --config.verify-deps-before-run=false exec prisma format --schema=prisma/schema/`; `pnpm --config.verify-deps-before-run=false exec prisma generate --schema=prisma/schema/`; `pnpm --config.verify-deps-before-run=false exec prisma validate --schema=prisma/schema/`; focused `pnpm --config.verify-deps-before-run=false exec vitest run src/lib/pharmacy/qr-draft-fingerprint.test.ts src/lib/pharmacy/qr-patient-match.test.ts src/lib/prescription/prescription-date-window.test.ts src/lib/validations/prescription.test.ts src/app/api/qr-scan-drafts/route.test.ts 'src/app/api/qr-scan-drafts/[id]/confirm/route.test.ts' src/app/api/prescription-intakes/route.test.ts src/server/services/prescription-intake-service.test.ts`; expanded `pnpm --config.verify-deps-before-run=false exec vitest run src/lib/pharmacy/qr-draft-fingerprint.test.ts src/lib/pharmacy/qr-patient-match.test.ts src/lib/prescription/prescription-date-window.test.ts src/lib/validations/prescription.test.ts src/lib/pharmacy/jahis-qr.test.ts src/lib/pharmacy/__tests__/jahis-qr.test.ts src/lib/pharmacy/jahis-supplemental-records-view.test.ts src/lib/api/request-body.test.ts src/lib/api/response-body.test.ts src/lib/api/route-params.test.ts src/app/api/prescription-intakes/route.test.ts 'src/app/api/prescription-intakes/[id]/route.test.ts' src/app/api/prescription-intakes/facility-batch/route.test.ts src/app/api/qr-scan-drafts/route.test.ts 'src/app/api/qr-scan-drafts/[id]/route.test.ts' 'src/app/api/qr-scan-drafts/[id]/confirm/route.test.ts' src/server/services/prescription-intake-service.test.ts src/server/services/jahis-supplemental-records.test.ts`; targeted ESLint with `--max-warnings=0`; `pnpm --config.verify-deps-before-run=false exec tsc --noEmit --pretty false`; `git diff --check` on target files; verifier and medical-safety subagent reviews

### 20260601-085827

- current task: improve QR payload-hash migration stability for existing QR draft rows
- files inspected: `git status --short`, `.codex/ralph-state.md`, memory registry lines for CareViaX continuation, `prisma/migrations/20260601084500_add_qr_scan_draft_payload_hash/migration.sql`, `prisma/schema/prescription.prisma`, `src/lib/pharmacy/qr-draft-fingerprint.ts`, `src/app/api/qr-scan-drafts/route.ts`, and focused validation outputs
- files changed: `prisma/migrations/20260601084500_add_qr_scan_draft_payload_hash/migration.sql`, `.codex/ralph-state.md`. Existing unrelated dirty files were preserved
- bugs found: the QR payload-hash migration originally added the nullable column and active unique index but did not backfill existing `QrScanDraft.raw_qr_texts`, so active legacy rows could remain outside the new duplicate guard. A naive backfill could also make the unique index fail if active duplicate drafts already exist
- security risks found: reduced duplicate QR draft risk for legacy data by backfilling canonical payload hashes for existing JSON-array rows. For existing active duplicates, the migration assigns the hash only to the first active row per org/hash so the partial unique index can still be created and future duplicates are blocked instead of failing the migration
- performance issues found: existing rows gain an indexed `qr_payload_hash` where safe, allowing duplicate checks to use the same indexed guard for legacy and newly-created drafts
- validation commands: `pnpm --config.verify-deps-before-run=false exec prisma validate --schema=prisma/schema/`; `pnpm --config.verify-deps-before-run=false exec vitest run src/lib/pharmacy/qr-draft-fingerprint.test.ts src/app/api/qr-scan-drafts/route.test.ts 'src/app/api/qr-scan-drafts/[id]/confirm/route.test.ts' src/app/api/prescription-intakes/route.test.ts`; `git diff --check -- prisma/migrations/20260601084500_add_qr_scan_draft_payload_hash/migration.sql prisma/schema/prescription.prisma src/lib/pharmacy/qr-draft-fingerprint.ts src/lib/pharmacy/qr-draft-fingerprint.test.ts src/app/api/qr-scan-drafts/route.ts src/app/api/qr-scan-drafts/route.test.ts 'src/app/api/qr-scan-drafts/[id]/confirm/route.ts' 'src/app/api/qr-scan-drafts/[id]/confirm/route.test.ts' src/app/api/prescription-intakes/route.ts src/app/api/prescription-intakes/route.test.ts`

### 20260601-103526

- current task: harden shared cursor pagination clients against malformed JSON responses and non-finite page caps
- files inspected: `git status --short`, `.codex/ralph-state.md`, CareViaX memory continuation notes, Next route handler docs, `src/lib/api/request-body.ts`, `src/lib/api/response-body.ts`, `src/lib/api/response-body.test.ts`, raw API request-body and JSON parsing scans, `src/lib/api/cursor-pagination-client.ts`, `src/lib/api/cursor-pagination-client.test.ts`, `src/lib/api/paginated-client.ts`, `src/lib/api/paginated-client.test.ts`, `src/lib/api/keyset-cursor.ts`, `src/lib/api/keyset-cursor.test.ts`, current target diffs, and focused validation outputs
- files changed: `src/lib/api/cursor-pagination-client.ts`, `src/lib/api/cursor-pagination-client.test.ts`, `src/lib/api/paginated-client.ts`, `src/lib/api/paginated-client.test.ts`, `.codex/ralph-state.md`. Existing unrelated dirty files and earlier pagination limit/keyset hardening already present in the same target files were preserved
- bugs found: cursor pagination clients still parsed response bodies with raw `response.json()`, so malformed JSON responses could leak a native parse error instead of the caller-facing fetch error. `cursor-pagination-client` also only clamped `maxPages` with `Math.max`, so non-finite values such as `NaN` skipped fetches and produced a misleading fetch failure
- security risks found: malformed upstream/API responses are now treated as invalid response shape and mapped to controlled caller-facing errors rather than leaking parser internals. Existing HTTP status handling and metadata shape validation were not relaxed
- performance issues found: non-finite `maxPages` now normalizes to the default cap instead of skipping useful work or risking unbounded loop semantics. Both cursor clients reuse `readJsonResponseBody` to avoid repeated ad hoc JSON parse handling
- validation commands: `pnpm --config.verify-deps-before-run=false exec prettier --write src/lib/api/cursor-pagination-client.ts src/lib/api/cursor-pagination-client.test.ts src/lib/api/paginated-client.ts src/lib/api/paginated-client.test.ts`; `pnpm --config.verify-deps-before-run=false exec vitest run src/lib/api/cursor-pagination-client.test.ts src/lib/api/paginated-client.test.ts src/lib/api/response-body.test.ts`; `pnpm --config.verify-deps-before-run=false exec eslint src/lib/api/cursor-pagination-client.ts src/lib/api/cursor-pagination-client.test.ts src/lib/api/paginated-client.ts src/lib/api/paginated-client.test.ts src/lib/api/response-body.ts src/lib/api/response-body.test.ts --max-warnings=0`; `pnpm --config.verify-deps-before-run=false exec tsc --noEmit --pretty false`; `git diff --check -- src/lib/api/cursor-pagination-client.ts src/lib/api/cursor-pagination-client.test.ts src/lib/api/paginated-client.ts src/lib/api/paginated-client.test.ts src/lib/api/response-body.ts src/lib/api/response-body.test.ts`

### 20260601-103722

- current task: harden distributed rate-limit external JSON response parsing without changing fail-closed semantics
- files inspected: `git status --short`, `.codex/ralph-state.md`, remaining response JSON scan output, `src/lib/api/response-body.ts`, `src/lib/api/response-body.test.ts`, `src/lib/api/rate-limit.ts`, `src/lib/api/rate-limit.test.ts`, current target diffs, and focused validation outputs
- files changed: `src/lib/api/rate-limit.ts`, `src/lib/api/rate-limit.test.ts`, `.codex/ralph-state.md`. Existing unrelated dirty files and earlier credential/counter validation hardening already present in the same target files were preserved
- bugs found: distributed rate limiting still parsed ECS container credentials and DynamoDB UpdateItem responses with raw `response.json()`. Invalid JSON was caught by the production fail-closed wrapper, but the logged cause was a low-level parser error rather than the route's explicit credential/counter validation failure
- security risks found: production fail-closed behavior remains intact for malformed credential and DynamoDB responses. - security risks found: malformed or unsafe PDF size-limit configuration now falls back to the built-in cap before ZIP creation or generated-file storage. Existing permission checks, patient access checks, queue conflict handling, audit writes, and generated-file cleanup behavior were not relaxed
- performance issues found: unsafe size-limit values no longer turn into a zero cap that wastes PDF rendering work by failing every non-empty export, and unsafe huge finite values no longer disable the bounded total-PDF-size guard

### 20260601-112515

- current task: harden Prisma database pool size environment parsing before adapter initialization
- files inspected: `git status --short`, `.codex/ralph-state.md`, DB client and import call-site scans, `src/lib/db/client.ts`, `src/lib/db/client.test.ts`, `docs/operations/perf-baseline-2026-03-31.md`, raw numeric/env scan output, current target diffs, and focused validation outputs
- files changed: `src/lib/db/client.ts`, `src/lib/db/client.test.ts`, `.codex/ralph-state.md`. Existing unrelated dirty files were preserved
- bugs found: `DATABASE_POOL_SIZE` was converted with plain `Number(...)` at module initialization, so `NaN`, `Infinity`, zero, negative, decimal, or very large values could be passed directly into the Prisma pg adapter as `max`
- security risks found: malformed DB pool configuration now fails closed to the default pool size before Prisma adapter construction. This reduces accidental resource exhaustion from oversized values while preserving the existing `DATABASE_URL` required check and Prisma global singleton behavior
- performance issues found: DB pool size now normalizes to an integer, defaults to 20 on invalid values, and caps at 100 to avoid typo-driven excessive connection pool sizes. Valid decimal values are truncated rather than forwarded as fractional pool sizes

### 20260601-113037

- current task: harden drug-master import fetch policy numeric boundaries and centralize keyset cursor JSON parsing
- files inspected: `git status --short`, `.codex/ralph-state.md`, CareViaX continuation memory notes, raw numeric/env scan output, `src/server/services/drug-master-import/shared.ts`, `src/server/services/drug-master-import/shared.test.ts`, drug-master import fetch call-site scans, `src/lib/api/keyset-cursor.ts`, `src/lib/api/keyset-cursor.test.ts`, `src/lib/db/json.ts`, `src/lib/utils/timeout.ts`, current target diffs, and focused validation outputs
- files changed: `src/server/services/drug-master-import/shared.ts`, `src/server/services/drug-master-import/shared.test.ts`, `src/lib/api/keyset-cursor.ts`, `src/lib/api/keyset-cursor.test.ts`, `.codex/ralph-state.md`. Existing unrelated dirty files and earlier keyset cursor object-shape hardening already present in the same target files were preserved
- bugs found: drug-master import fetch policies accepted non-finite or unsafe `timeoutMs`, `maxRedirects`, and `maxBytes` values directly in timer, redirect-loop, and response-size code paths. `maxRedirects: Infinity` could remove the redirect-loop bound, `maxBytes: Infinity` could disable the policy byte limit, and malformed timeout values could alter external fetch abort behavior. Keyset cursor decoding still used direct `JSON.parse` despite having a shared null-on-failure JSON parser
- security risks found: official drug-master import fetches now normalize timeout configuration through `normalizePositiveTimeoutMs`, clamp redirect limits to a bounded 0..10 range, and cap requested byte limits to the source policy limit before reading external responses. Existing HTTPS-only URL validation, allowed-host validation, DNS private/reserved IP rejection, redirect revalidation, `cache: no-store`, body streaming cap checks, and ZIP expansion limits were not relaxed. Malformed keyset cursor JSON now flows through `parseJsonObjectOrNull` before date/id validation
- performance issues found: malformed redirect and byte-limit policies can no longer create unbounded redirect loops or unbounded response reads. Invalid timeout values fall back to the existing 15s fetch timeout instead of producing immediate aborts, excessive waits, or timer overflow behavior
- validation commands: `pnpm --config.verify-deps-before-run=false exec prettier --write src/server/services/drug-master-import/shared.ts src/server/services/drug-master-import/shared.test.ts`; `pnpm --config.verify-deps-before-run=false exec vitest run src/server/services/drug-master-import/shared.test.ts src/lib/utils/timeout.test.ts`; `pnpm --config.verify-deps-before-run=false exec eslint src/server/services/drug-master-import/shared.ts src/server/services/drug-master-import/shared.test.ts src/lib/utils/timeout.ts src/lib/utils/timeout.test.ts --max-warnings=0`; `pnpm --config.verify-deps-before-run=false exec tsc --noEmit --pretty false`; `git diff --check -- src/server/services/drug-master-import/shared.ts src/server/services/drug-master-import/shared.test.ts src/lib/utils/timeout.ts src/lib/utils/timeout.test.ts`; `pnpm --config.verify-deps-before-run=false exec prettier --write src/lib/api/keyset-cursor.ts src/lib/api/keyset-cursor.test.ts src/server/services/drug-master-import/shared.ts src/server/services/drug-master-import/shared.test.ts`; `pnpm --config.verify-deps-before-run=false exec vitest run src/server/services/drug-master-import/shared.test.ts src/lib/api/keyset-cursor.test.ts src/lib/db/json.test.ts src/lib/utils/timeout.test.ts`; `pnpm --config.verify-deps-before-run=false exec eslint src/server/services/drug-master-import/shared.ts src/server/services/drug-master-import/shared.test.ts src/lib/api/keyset-cursor.ts src/lib/api/keyset-cursor.test.ts src/lib/db/json.ts src/lib/db/json.test.ts src/lib/utils/timeout.ts src/lib/utils/timeout.test.ts --max-warnings=0`; final `pnpm --config.verify-deps-before-run=false exec tsc --noEmit --pretty false`; final `git diff --check -- src/server/services/drug-master-import/shared.ts src/server/services/drug-master-import/shared.test.ts src/lib/api/keyset-cursor.ts src/lib/api/keyset-cursor.test.ts src/lib/db/json.ts src/lib/db/json.test.ts src/lib/utils/timeout.ts src/lib/utils/timeout.test.ts`

- bugs found: patient lab GET parsed the external `limit` query with `parseInt` and only upper-clamped it, so malformed, zero, negative, or non-finite-equivalent strings could derive unsafe Prisma `take` values or accidental partial-number limits
- security risks found: malformed lab-list limits now normalize before any patient lab read. Existing authentication, canVisit permission check, patient assignment scoping, analyte-code validation, and patient-not-found behavior were not relaxed
- performance issues found: lab-list queries now use a bounded 1..200 integer limit with a default of 50, preventing malformed or oversized query parameters from issuing invalid or unexpectedly large reads. The route reuses the shared pagination integer parser instead of duplicating numeric parsing

- bugs found: residual-medication GET accepted any positive finite `limit` and could pass huge values directly to Prisma `take`. Drug-master import logs accepted decimal limits such as `10.5`, which could also be forwarded to Prisma as a non-integer `take`
- security risks found: residual-medication list limits now normalize before patient/visit-scoped residual reads, preserving existing canVisit auth, org scoping, visit-record assignment checks, inaccessible-resource empty response behavior, and POST validation behavior. Drug-master import log limits now normalize before import-log reads without relaxing source/status validation
- performance issues found: specified residual-medication limits now default to 50 on malformed values and cap at 200; omitted residual-medication limits keep the existing unbounded read behavior. Drug-master import log limits now use a strict integer parser with default 10 and max 50, preventing malformed or decimal values from reaching Prisma

### 20260601-115130

- current task: harden dashboard medication deadline and reject-reason stats day-window parsing before date-range queries
- files inspected: `git status --short`, `.codex/ralph-state.md`, CareViaX continuation memory notes, Next route handler docs in `node_modules/next/dist/docs/01-app/03-api-reference/03-file-conventions/route.md`, Vercel Next.js skill notes, `src/app/api/dashboard/medication-deadlines/route.ts`, `src/app/api/dashboard/medication-deadlines/route.test.ts`, `src/app/api/admin/reject-reason-stats/route.ts`, `src/app/api/admin/reject-reason-stats/route.test.ts`, `src/lib/api/pagination.ts`, `src/lib/api/pagination.test.ts`, current target diffs, and focused validation outputs
- files changed: `src/app/api/dashboard/medication-deadlines/route.ts`, `src/app/api/dashboard/medication-deadlines/route.test.ts`, `src/app/api/admin/reject-reason-stats/route.ts`, `src/app/api/admin/reject-reason-stats/route.test.ts`, `src/lib/api/pagination.ts`, `.codex/ralph-state.md`. Existing unrelated dirty files and earlier shared pagination export already present in `src/lib/api/pagination.ts` were preserved
- bugs found: medication deadline `within_days` used `parseInt` directly and could produce `NaN`, partial-number windows, negative windows, or very large date ranges before querying visit schedules. Reject-reason stats similarly used `parseInt` plus only an upper clamp, so malformed values could produce `Invalid Date` filters and partial-number periods
- security risks found: day-window query parameters now normalize before org-scoped dashboard schedule reads or dispense audit reads. Existing withAuth permission checks, org scoping, schedule status filtering, dispense result filtering, and response aggregation semantics were not relaxed
- performance issues found: medication deadline windows now default to 7 on malformed values and clamp to 0..365 days; reject-reason stats periods now default to 30 on malformed values and clamp to 0..365 days. This prevents typo-driven broad date scans and invalid-date queries while preserving a same-day window with `0`
- validation commands: `pnpm --config.verify-deps-before-run=false exec prettier --write src/app/api/dashboard/medication-deadlines/route.ts src/app/api/dashboard/medication-deadlines/route.test.ts src/app/api/admin/reject-reason-stats/route.ts src/app/api/admin/reject-reason-stats/route.test.ts src/lib/api/pagination.ts`; `pnpm --config.verify-deps-before-run=false exec vitest run src/app/api/dashboard/medication-deadlines/route.test.ts src/app/api/admin/reject-reason-stats/route.test.ts src/lib/api/pagination.test.ts`; `pnpm --config.verify-deps-before-run=false exec eslint src/app/api/dashboard/medication-deadlines/route.ts src/app/api/dashboard/medication-deadlines/route.test.ts src/app/api/admin/reject-reason-stats/route.ts src/app/api/admin/reject-reason-stats/route.test.ts src/lib/api/pagination.ts src/lib/api/pagination.test.ts --max-warnings=0`; `pnpm --config.verify-deps-before-run=false exec tsc --noEmit --pretty false`; `git diff --check -- src/app/api/dashboard/medication-deadlines/route.ts src/app/api/dashboard/medication-deadlines/route.test.ts src/app/api/admin/reject-reason-stats/route.ts src/app/api/admin/reject-reason-stats/route.test.ts src/lib/api/pagination.ts .codex/ralph-state.md`
